title: The temptation of Jesus part 9
scripture_text: ‘’Then Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil. After fasting forty days and forty nights, he was hungry. The tempter came to him and said, “If you are the Son of God, tell these stones to become bread.” Jesus answered, “It is written: ‘Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.’ Then the devil took him to the holy city and had him stand on the highest point of the temple. “If you are the Son of God,’’ he said, “throw yourself down. For it is written: “‘He will command his angels concerning you, and they will lift you up in their hands, so that you will not strike your foot against a stone.’ Jesus answered him, ‘’It is also written: ‘Do not put the Lord your God to the test.’ ’Again, the devil took him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their splendour “All this I will give you,” he said, “if you will bow down and worship me.” Jesus said to him, “Away from me, Satan! For it is written: ‘Worship the Lord your God, and serve him only.’ Then the devil left him, and angels came and attended him.
devotional:
Satan wanted Jesus to worship him.

Satan is trying to change the orientation of Jesus’ life by changing what he lived for, or to put it in biblical terms, Satan wanted to change the object of Jesus,’ worship. By bowing down to Satan, Jesus would have been coming under his authority. Worship is the foundation on which you build your life. Now, notice we are not saying that worship should be the foundation but worship is the foundation on which you are building your life. We are all worshipping something. Every person has something or someone at its centre that is worshipped. Now remember, the intention of Satan behind temptations is to rob us of our identity, to cause us to think, feel and live as people who have no life changing experience of God as our Father. So to accomplish this, he offers us an easier life so he can change what we worship. There are many subtle and discreet ways of bowing down to Satan and worshipping him; it is rarely an overt tangible act, and is usually expressed though idolatry.

What we worship is the thing that defines who we are and how we live.

We serve what we worship. What we worship is the thing that reveals most about us. What we worship dictates the terms of our lives. To worship is to give yourself to it.

If we worship sex, we will run to sexual pleasure for comfort, significance and value, we won’t feel loved, valued or complete without sex.

If we worship power then we will have a desperate need to be in control, so you gossip or stab people in the back with cruel words so you can hold onto a position and power.

If we worship money, then we will relate our significance and value to what we own and possess.

Here is the most crucial thing we are going to say. Our experience and enjoyment as children of God is totally dependant on our worship of God. Our identity as children of God has created within us an unceasing need to be worshippers of God. Satan knows that once we set our hearts on something else, once we cease to have a heart that recognises our need to worship God alone, then we will be open to his suggestions, his purposes and ways.

Jesus was offered all the kingdoms of the world, a crown without a cross, glory without pain, an inheritance without death, but he refused, because he wanted to worship and serve His Father alone. Those who worship God from the heart, will make a difference in the world in which they live.
prayer: Father thank you that to love and worship you, is the greatest honour of our lives.
html:
The temptation of Jesus part 9

Study Text: Matthew 4:1-11
‘’Then Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil. After fasting forty days and forty nights, he was hungry. The tempter came to him and said, “If you are the Son of God, tell these stones to become bread.” Jesus answered, “It is written: ‘Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.’ Then the devil took him to the holy city and had him stand on the highest point of the temple. “If you are the Son of God,’’ he said, “throw yourself down. For it is written: “‘He will command his angels concerning you, and they will lift you up in their hands, so that you will not strike your foot against a stone.’ Jesus answered him, ‘’It is also written: ‘Do not put the Lord your God to the test.’ ’Again, the devil took him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their splendour “All this I will give you,” he said, “if you will bow down and worship me.” Jesus said to him, “Away from me, Satan! For it is written: ‘Worship the Lord your God, and serve him only.’ Then the devil left him, and angels came and attended him.

Satan wanted Jesus to worship him.

Satan is trying to change the orientation of Jesus’ life by changing what he lived for, or to put it in biblical terms, Satan wanted to change the object of Jesus,’ worship. By bowing down to Satan, Jesus would have been coming under his authority. Worship is the foundation on which you build your life. Now, notice we are not saying that worship should be the foundation but worship is the foundation on which you are building your life. We are all worshipping something. Every person has something or someone at its centre that is worshipped. Now remember, the intention of Satan behind temptations is to rob us of our identity, to cause us to think, feel and live as people who have no life changing experience of God as our Father. So to accomplish this, he offers us an easier life so he can change what we worship. There are many subtle and discreet ways of bowing down to Satan and worshipping him; it is rarely an overt tangible act, and is usually expressed though idolatry.

What we worship is the thing that defines who we are and how we live.

We serve what we worship. What we worship is the thing that reveals most about us. What we worship dictates the terms of our lives. To worship is to give yourself to it.

If we worship sex, we will run to sexual pleasure for comfort, significance and value, we won’t feel loved, valued or complete without sex.

If we worship power then we will have a desperate need to be in control, so you gossip or stab people in the back with cruel words so you can hold onto a position and power.

If we worship money, then we will relate our significance and value to what we own and possess.

Here is the most crucial thing we are going to say. Our experience and enjoyment as children of God is totally dependant on our worship of God. Our identity as children of God has created within us an unceasing need to be worshippers of God. Satan knows that once we set our hearts on something else, once we cease to have a heart that recognises our need to worship God alone, then we will be open to his suggestions, his purposes and ways.

Jesus was offered all the kingdoms of the world, a crown without a cross, glory without pain, an inheritance without death, but he refused, because he wanted to worship and serve His Father alone. Those who worship God from the heart, will make a difference in the world in which they live.

Prayer: Father thank you that to love and worship you, is the greatest honour of our lives.
Bible in a year reading plan: 1Kings 8-9
